title: लक्ष्मीनारायण हृदयम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री लक्ष्मीनारायण हृदयम् ॥

॥ विनियोगः ॥
अस्य श्री लक्ष्मीनारायण हृदय स्तोत्र मन्त्रस्य
भार्गव ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः।
श्री लक्ष्मीनारायणो देवता।
ॐ बीजम्। श्रीं शक्तिः। ह्रीं कीलकम्।
श्री लक्ष्मीनारायण प्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
लक्ष्मीनारायणं देवं जगत्सृष्टिकरं प्रभुम्।
ध्यायेत्पद्मासनासीनं सर्वालंकारभूषितम्॥
शङ्खचक्रगदापद्मधारिणं पीतवाससम्।
श्रिया सहासनासीनं ध्यायेल्लक्ष्मीपतिं हरिम्॥

॥ श्री नारायण हृदयम् ॥

ॐ नमो नारायणाय हृदयाय नमः।
ॐ नमो नारायणाय शिरसे स्वाहा।
ॐ नमो नारायणाय शिखायै वषट्।
ॐ नमो नारायणाय कवचाय हुम्।
ॐ नमो नारायणाय नेत्रत्रयाय वौषट्।
ॐ नमो नारायणाय अस्त्राय फट्॥

नारायणः परं ज्योतिरात्मा नारायणः परः।
नारायणः परं ब्रह्म तत्त्वं नारायणः परः॥१॥

नारायणः परो ध्याता ध्यानं नारायणः परः।
यच्च किञ्चिज्जगत्सर्वं दृश्यते श्रूयतेऽपि वा॥२॥

अन्तर्बहिश्च तत्सर्वं व्याप्य नारायणः स्थितः।
अनन्तमव्ययं काव्यं समुद्रेऽन्तः स्थितं विभुम्॥३॥

पद्मनाभं सुरेशानं विश्वधारणमव्ययम्।
विश्वस्य निखिलाधारं ध्यायेन्नारायणं सदा॥४॥

मत्स्यं कूर्मं वराहं च नारसिंहं च वामनम्।
रामं रामं च कृष्णं च बुद्धं कल्किनमेव च॥५॥

दशावतारमीशानं दयानिधिमनुत्तमम्।
वन्दे विष्णुं भवभयहरं सर्वलोकैकनाथम्॥६॥

॥ श्री लक्ष्मी हृदयम् ॥

ॐ श्रीं ह्रीं श्री महालक्ष्म्यै हृदयाय नमः।
ॐ श्रीं ह्रीं श्री महालक्ष्म्यै शिरसे स्वाहा।
ॐ श्रीं ह्रीं श्री महालक्ष्म्यै शिखायै वषट्।
ॐ श्रीं ह्रीं श्री महालक्ष्म्यै कवचाय हुम्।
ॐ श्रीं ह्रीं श्री महालक्ष्म्यै नेत्रत्रयाय वौषट्।
ॐ श्रीं ह्रीं श्री महालक्ष्म्यै अस्त्राय फट्॥

पद्मानने पद्मपदे पद्मप्रिये सुरपूजिते।
पद्मदलायताक्षि च पद्मिनि पद्महस्ते॥७॥

लक्ष्मीर्यस्य परिग्रहः स हि सुखी धन्यश्च पुण्यश्च सः।
लक्ष्मीर्यं प्रति शोभते प्रतिदिनं सद्वासरं तस्य हि॥८॥

लक्ष्मीर्यस्य गृहे वसेत्पुरुषवद्दारैश्च मातापितृ-
भ्रातृभ्यश्च बलं च कीर्तिरतुला तस्यैव दत्ता भवेत्॥९॥

सत्यं सत्यं पुनः सत्यमुद्धृत्य भुजमुच्यते।
वेदाच्छास्त्रं परं नास्ति न देवः केशवात्परः॥१०॥

लक्ष्मीनारायणौ देवौ ध्यात्वा युगपदीश्वरौ।
नारायणस्य हृदयं लक्ष्म्या हृदयमेव च॥११॥

य इदं पठते नित्यं प्रातरुत्थाय मानवः।
सर्वान् कामानवाप्नोति लक्ष्मीनारायणप्रसादतः॥१२॥

धनधान्यसमृद्धिश्च सौभाग्यं पुत्रलाभकम्।
बलं तेजो यशो दैर्घ्यं स्वास्थ्यमायुश्च विन्दति॥१३

॥ इति अथर्ववेदोक्तं श्री लक्ष्मीनारायण हृदयं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Lakshmi-Narayana Hridayam ||

|| Viniyogah ||
Asya Shri Lakshmi-Narayana Hridaya Stotra Mantrasya
Bhargava Rishiph. Anushtup Chhandah.
Shri Lakshmi-Narayano Devata.
Om Bijam. Shrim Shaktih. Hrim Kilakam.
Shri Lakshmi-Narayana Prityarthe Jape Viniyogah.

|| Dhyanam ||
Lakshmi-Narayanam Devam Jagat-srishti-karam Prabhum.
Dhyayet-padmasana-asinam Sarvalamkara-bhushitam.
Shankha-chakra-gada-padma-dharinam Pita-vasasam.
Shriya Sahasa-na-asinam Dhyayel-Lakshmi-patim Harim.

|| Shri Narayana Hridayam ||

Om Namo Narayanaya Hridayaya Namah.
Om Namo Narayanaya Shirase Svaha.
Om Namo Narayanaya Shikhayai Vashat.
Om Namo Narayanaya Kavachaya Hum.
Om Namo Narayanaya Netra-trayaya Vaushat.
Om Namo Narayanaya Astraya Phat.

Narayanah Param Jyotir-atma Narayanah Parah.
Narayanah Param Brahma Tattvam Narayanah Parah.||1||

Narayanah Paro Dhyata Dhyanam Narayanah Parah.
Yach-cha Kinchij-jagat-sarvam Drishyate Shruyate'pi Va.||2||

Antar-bahish-cha Tat-sarvam Vyapya Narayanah Sthitah.
Anantam-avyayam Kavyam Samudre'ntah Sthitam Vibhum.||3||

Padma-nabham Sura-ishanam Vishva-dharanam-avyayam.
Vishvasya Nikhila-adharam Dhyayen-Narayanam Sada.||4||

Matsyam Kurmam Varaham cha Narasimham cha Vamanam.
Ramam Ramam cha Krishnam cha Buddham Kalkinam-eva cha.||5||

Dasha-avataram-ishanam Daya-nidhim-anuttamam.
Vande Vishnum Bhava-bhaya-haram Sarva-lokaika-natham.||6||

|| Shri Lakshmi Hridayam ||

Om Shrim Hrim Shri Mahalakshmyai Hridayaya Namah.
Om Shrim Hrim Shri Mahalakshmyai Shirase Svaha.
Om Shrim Hrim Shri Mahalakshmyai Shikhayai Vashat.
Om Shrim Hrim Shri Mahalakshmyai Kavachaya Hum.
Om Shrim Hrim Shri Mahalakshmyai Netra-trayaya Vaushat.
Om Shrim Hrim Shri Mahalakshmyai Astraya Phat.

Padma-nane Padma-pade Padma-priye Sura-pujite.
Padma-dalayata-akshi cha Padmini Padma-haste.||7||

Lakshmih Yasya Parigrahah Sa Hi Sukhi Dhanyash-cha Punyash-cha Sah.
Lakshmih Yam Prati Shobhate Pratidinam Sad-vasaram Tasya Hi.||8||

Lakshmih Yasya Grihe Vaset Purusha-vad-daraish-cha Mata-pitri-
Bhratribhyash-cha Balam cha Kirtir-atula Tasyaiva Datta Bhavet.||9||

Satyam Satyam Punah Satyam-uddhritya Bhujam-uchyate.
Vedach-chhastram Param Nasti Na Devah Keshavat-parah.||10||

Lakshmi-Narayanau Devau Dhyatva Yugapad-ishvarau.
Narayanasya Hridayam Lakshmya Hridayam-eva cha.||11||

Ya Idam Pathate Nityam Pratar-utthaya Manavah.
Sarvan Kaman-avapnoti Lakshmi-Narayana-prasadatah.||12||

Dhana-dhanya-samriddhish-cha Saubhagyam Putra-labhakam.
Balam Tejo Yasho Dairghyam Svasthyam-ayush-cha Vindati.||13||

|| Iti Atharva-vedoktam Shri Lakshmi-Narayana Hridayam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
श्री नारायण हृदयम् — ॐ नमो नारायणाय — नारायण ही परम ज्योति हैं, नारायण ही परमात्मा हैं, नारायण ही परम ब्रह्म हैं, नारायण ही परम तत्त्व हैं। जो कुछ भी इस जगत में दृश्य या श्रव्य है, उस सबको अन्तर और बाहर व्याप्त करके नारायण स्थित हैं।

पद्मनाभ, सुरेश, विश्वधारण, अव्यय — ऐसे नारायण का सदा ध्यान करें। मत्स्य, कूर्म, वराह, नरसिंह, वामन, राम, कृष्ण, बुद्ध, कल्कि — दशावतारों के ईश्वर, दयानिधि, अनुत्तम विष्णु को वन्दन।

श्री लक्ष्मी हृदयम् — जिसकी पत्नी लक्ष्मी हैं वह सुखी है, धन्य है, पुण्यवान है। जिसके प्रति लक्ष्मी शोभायमान हैं उसका प्रतिदिन शुभ है। जिसके घर में लक्ष्मी निवास करती हैं उसे पत्नी, माता-पिता, भाइयों से बल और अतुल कीर्ति प्राप्त होती है।

सत्य-सत्य-पुनः सत्य — वेद से श्रेष्ठ शास्त्र नहीं, केशव से श्रेष्ठ देवता नहीं।

जो प्रातःकाल उठकर नित्य इसका पाठ करता है उसे लक्ष्मीनारायण की कृपा से सभी कामनाएँ, धन-धान्य, सौभाग्य, पुत्र, बल, तेज, यश, दीर्घायु और स्वास्थ्य प्राप्त होता है।

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीलक्ष्मीनारायणहृदयस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य भार्गव (भृगु) ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीलक्ष्मीनारायणो देवता। ॐ बीजम्। श्रीं शक्तिः। ह्रीं कीलकम्। श्रीलक्ष्मीनारायणप्रीत्यर्थे लक्ष्मीनारायणहृदयजपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Invokes the combined grace of both Lakshmi and Narayana simultaneously
Invokes wealth, progeny, strength, radiance, fame, longevity, and health
Contains both Narayana Hridayam and Lakshmi Hridayam in one recitation
Reciting daily in the morning fulfils all desires by divine grace
Establishes permanent presence of Lakshmi in the devotee's home and family
Invokes the blessing of both material prosperity and spiritual liberation
description: The Lakshmi Narayana Hridayam, known in Sanskrit as लक्ष्मीनारायण हृदयम्, is a heart-essence prayer in praise of Lord Vishnu. This revered text originates from Atharva Veda (Khila Sukta tradition). Devotees recite this prayer to establish permanent presence of Lakshmi in the devotee's home and family and grant wealth, progeny, strength, radiance, fame, longevity, and health. It is also said to bestow both material prosperity and spiritual liberation. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Friday (Shukravar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Diwali and Ekadashi. With 13 verses and a reading time of about 12 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Lakshmi Narayana Hridayam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Lakshmi Narayana Hridayam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Combined heart-essence hymn of Vishnu and Lakshmi for wealth and spiritual grace.
relatedSearches: Lakshmi Narayana Hridayam Sanskrit lyrics PDF
Lakshmi Hridayam and Narayana Hridayam recited together
Atharva Rahasya source of Lakshmi-Narayana Hridayam
Bhargava Bhrigu Maharishi Vishnu Lakshmi devotee
Lakshmi Narayana Hridayam benefits for wealth

seoFaqs.question: What is the Lakshmi-Narayana Hridayam and what makes it unique?
seoFaqs.answer: The Lakshmi-Narayana Hridayam is a famous combined heart-essence prayer that unites the Narayana Hridayam (heart of Vishnu) and the Lakshmi Hridayam (heart of Lakshmi) into a single recitation. It is traditionally attributed to Bhargava (Maharshi Bhrigu) as the Rishi, with anushtup chhandas, Sri Lakshmi-Narayana as the devata, Om as the bija, Shrim as the shakti and Hrim as the kilaka. The text declares itself to be from the Atharva Veda Rahasya tradition (Atharva Vedoktam) and is one of the most popular daily recitations in Vaishnava households for combined material prosperity and spiritual liberation. (Sources: traditional Vaishnava manuals; Sri Vaishnava household practice.)

seoFaqs.question: Why are the Lakshmi Hridayam and Narayana Hridayam always recited together?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 11 of the text itself states Lakshmi-Narayanau devau dhyatva yugapad-ishvarau, Narayanasya hridayam Lakshmya hridayam-eva cha — meaning meditate on the divine couple Lakshmi and Narayana simultaneously, for the heart of Narayana is itself the heart of Lakshmi. Tradition therefore prescribes that the Narayana Hridayam should always be recited first, followed immediately by the Lakshmi Hridayam, then concluded with another recitation of the Narayana Hridayam — sandwiching Lakshmi within Narayana. This is symbolic of the inseparability of the divine couple, where Lakshmi is Narayana's eternal consort dwelling in his chest as Shrivatsa and never separated even for a moment. (Sources: traditional recitation order; Vaishnava acharya teachings.)

seoFaqs.question: Who was Bhargava the rishi and what is his connection to this text?
seoFaqs.answer: Bhargava refers to Maharshi Bhrigu, the great rishi of the Bhrigu lineage, mind-born son of Brahma, husband of Khyati and father of Lakshmi (in some Puranic accounts), Chyavana, Shukra and Kavi. He is famous for the Bhrigu test of the Trinity recorded in the Bhagavata Purana, where he ultimately recognised Vishnu as the supreme due to Vishnu's forgiveness of his kick on the chest. As Lakshmi's father in puranic genealogy and as the rishi who tested and chose Vishnu, Bhrigu is considered the natural seer (drashta) of this combined Lakshmi-Narayana hymn. The Bhrigu Samhita of jyotisha is also attributed to him. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana; Mahabharata; Vishnu Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the recommended order and time for daily recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 12 specifies that one who recites this text after rising in the morning (pratar-utthaya) obtains all desires through the grace of Lakshmi-Narayana. The traditional sequence is: bath, sandhyavandanam, dhyana of the Lakshmi-Narayana couple in their iconographic form (seated on padmasana, holding shankha-chakra-gada-padma in pitambara, with Lakshmi by the side), recitation of the Narayana Hridayam, recitation of the Lakshmi Hridayam, second recitation of the Narayana Hridayam (Lakshmi sandwich), and then daily worship. Friday is the day specially recommended (sacred to Lakshmi), and Ekadashi, Diwali, Akshaya Tritiya and Vaikuntha Ekadashi are the principal festival days. (Sources: Vaishnava daily anushthana manuals.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Lakshmi-Narayana Hridayam promise?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 13 lists the boons explicitly: dhana-dhanya-samriddhi (wealth and grain abundance), saubhagya (marital and household auspiciousness), putra-labha (progeny), bala (strength), tejas (radiance), yashas (fame), dairghya (longevity), svasthya (good health) and ayus (life-span). Verse 8 declares that the household where Lakshmi resides becomes happy, blessed and meritorious; verse 9 adds that the family receives strength and incomparable fame in the form of spouse, parents and brothers. The crowning benefit is the combined attainment of bhukti (worldly enjoyment) and mukti (spiritual liberation) — a rare combination promised in only a few stotras. (Sources: text phalashruti verses 8-13.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Atharva Rahasya tradition and is this text actually in the Atharva Veda?
seoFaqs.answer: The text closes with Iti Atharva-vedoktam (thus declared in the Atharva Veda) and is grouped under the Atharva Rahasya category — a body of supplementary mystical and tantric texts traditionally attached to the Atharva Veda but not part of its core Samhita. Modern Vedic scholarship classifies such texts as Khila (supplementary) or Pancharatra Agama compositions that adopted the Atharva-attribution to claim Vedic authority — a common practice in classical India. Their authority for Vaishnava traditionalists rests not on academic Vedic chronology but on continuous lineage transmission. The text remains the most important combined Lakshmi-Vishnu hridayam regardless of its precise Vedic dating. (Sources: Pancharatra studies; Atharva Khila tradition.)
